use_case_name: When cable disconnected on EV-side: Suspend Transaction

structured.name: When cable disconnected on EV-side: Suspend Transaction
structured.objective: To suspend an ongoing transaction when the Charging Cable is unplugged on the EV side.
structured.description:
This use case covers how a transaction is suspended when the EV Driver unplugs the cable at the
EV side. In this use case the Configuration Variable: StopTxOnEVSideDisconnect = false.
The Charging Cable is unplugged at the EV side. This is detected by the Charging Station. The
Charging Station stops the energy offering (safety), but does not stop the transaction. The
Charging Cable, if locked, will remain locked at the Charging Station until the EV Driver returns and
presents his/hers IdToken.
scenario: 1. EV Driver unplugs the cable at the EV while a transaction is ongoing.

2. The energy offer is suspended.
When the EV Driver plugs the cable back in, the transaction is resumed.
A

1. The Charging Station sends a TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Updated, trigger =
CablePluggedIn)
A

2. The CSMS responds with a TransactionEventResponse.
If cable not permanently attached
B

1. The EV Driver is authorized by the Charging Station and/or CSMS to unlock the charging
cable.
B

3. The Charging Station sends a TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Ended, trigger =
StopAuthorized).
B

4. The EV Driver removes the charging cable.
B

5. The Charging Station sends a StatusNotificationRequest to the CSMS with the status
Available.
B

6. The CSMS responds with a StatusNotificationResponse.
If cable permanently attached
C

1. The cable is not plugged in within timeout.
C

2. The Charging Station sends a TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Ended, trigger =
EVCommunicationLost, stoppedReason = EVDisconnected).
C

3. The Charging Station sends a StatusNotificationRequest to the CSMS with the status
Available.
C
4. The CSMS responds with a StatusNotificationResponse.
structured.prerequisites:
Configuration Variable: StopTxOnEVSideDisconnect = false
A transaction is ongoing
structured.postconditions:
Successful postcondition:
The Charging Station is in Idle status.
The regular transaction is resumed.
Failure postcondition:
n/a

requirements.precondition:
If StopTxOnEVSideDisconnect =
false AND
Cable not permanently attached
requirements.definition:
The Connector SHALL remain locked at the
Charging Station until the EV Driver presents the
IdToken.

requirements.precondition:
If StopTxOnEVSideDisconnect =
false AND
Cable permanently attached AND
Cable not plugged in within timeout
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL deauthorize the
transaction.

requirements.precondition:
When a TransactionEventRequest has
to be created
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL set the message’s
seqNo field as specified in Sequence Number
Generation.

requirements.precondition:
When configured to send meter data
in the TransactionEventRequest
(eventType = Ended), See: Meter
Values - Configuration
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL add the configured
measurands to the optional meterValue field in the
TransactionEventRequest(eventType = Ended) sent
to the CSMS to provide more details about
transaction usage.

requirements.precondition:
E10.FR.04
AND
The Charging Station is running low on
memory
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station MAY drop meter data in the
TransactionEventRequest(eventType = Ended)
message.

When dropping meter data, the Charging Station
SHALL drop intermediate values first (2nd value,
4th value, 6th etc), not drop the start and end values
from the list.

The Charging Station SHALL retrieve signed meter
values and put them in the signedMeterValue field
of sampledValues.

chunks.content:
# E10 - When cable disconnected on EV-side: Suspend Transaction

**Objective:** To suspend an ongoing transaction when the Charging Cable is unplugged on the EV side.

**Description:** This use case covers how a transaction is suspended when the EV Driver unplugs the cable at the
EV side. In this use case the Configuration Variable: StopTxOnEVSideDisconnect = false.
The Charging Cable is unplugged at the EV side. This is detected by the Charging Station. The
Charging Station stops the energy offering (safety), but does not stop the transaction. The
Charging Cable, if locked, will remain locked at the Charging Station until the EV Driver returns and
presents his/hers IdToken.

**Actors:** Charging Station, CSMS, EV Driver

**Scenario:**
1. EV Driver unplugs the cable at the EV while a transaction is ongoing.
2. The energy offer is suspended.
When the EV Driver plugs the cable back in, the transaction is resumed.
A
1. The Charging Station sends a TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Updated, trigger =
CablePluggedIn)
A
2. The CSMS responds with a TransactionEventResponse.
If cable not permanently attached
B
1. The EV Driver is authorized by the Charging Station and/or CSMS to unlock the charging
cable.
B
2. The cable is unlocked.
B
3. The Charging Station sends a TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Ended, trigger =
StopAuthorized).
B
4. The EV Driver removes the charging cable.
B
5. The Charging Station sends a StatusNotificationRequest to the CSMS with the status
Available.
B
6. The CSMS responds with a StatusNotificationResponse.
If cable permanently attached
C
1. The cable is not plugged in within timeout.
C
2. The Charging Station sends a TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Ended, trigger =
EVCommunicationLost, stoppedReason = EVDisconnected).
C
3. The Charging Station sends a StatusNotificationRequest to the CSMS with the status
Available.
C
4. The CSMS responds with a StatusNotificationResponse.

**Prerequisites:** Configuration Variable: StopTxOnEVSideDisconnect = false
A transaction is ongoing

**Postconditions:** Successful postcondition:
The Charging Station is in Idle status.
The regular transaction is resumed.
Failure postcondition:
n/a

metadata.heading: E10 - When cable disconnected on EV-side: Suspend Transaction
metadata.use_case_name: When cable disconnected on EV-side: Suspend Transaction

chunks.content:
# E10 - Requirements

**E10.FR.01**
Precondition: If StopTxOnEVSideDisconnect =
false AND
Cable not permanently attached
The Connector SHALL remain locked at the
Charging Station until the EV Driver presents the
IdToken.

**E10.FR.02**
Precondition: If StopTxOnEVSideDisconnect =
false AND
Cable permanently attached AND
Cable not plugged in within timeout
The Charging Station SHALL deauthorize the
transaction.

**E10.FR.03**
Precondition: When a TransactionEventRequest has
to be created
The Charging Station SHALL set the message’s
seqNo field as specified in Sequence Number
Generation.

**E10.FR.04**
Precondition: When configured to send meter data
in the TransactionEventRequest
(eventType = Ended), See: Meter
Values - Configuration
The Charging Station SHALL add the configured
measurands to the optional meterValue field in the
TransactionEventRequest(eventType = Ended) sent
to the CSMS to provide more details about
transaction usage.

**E10.FR.05**
Precondition: E10.FR.04
AND
The Charging Station is running low on
memory
The Charging Station MAY drop meter data in the
TransactionEventRequest(eventType = Ended)
message.

**E10.FR.06**
Precondition: E10.FR.05
When dropping meter data, the Charging Station
SHALL drop intermediate values first (2nd value,
4th value, 6th etc), not drop the start and end values
from the list.

**E10.FR.07**
Precondition: SampledDataSignReadings is true
The Charging Station SHALL retrieve signed meter
values and put them in the signedMeterValue field
of sampledValues.

metadata.heading: E10 - Requirements
